tools.name: publish
tools.description: Publish HTML or Markdown content as a shareable URL. Pass `content` byte-for-byte from the source — do not retype, reformat, minify, summarize, or 'clean up' the input; even one stray character can break inline scripts on the published page. If the content is too large to copy verbatim through the m

tools.name: publish_site
tools.description: Publish a multi-file HTML site from a base64-encoded ZIP file. The ZIP must contain an index.html at its root. For sites larger than ~10MB — or whenever you have the file on disk — prefer the REST API /v1/artifacts/upload endpoint to avoid base64 overhead and to guarantee byte-faithful upload.

tools.name: get
tools.description: Get artifact metadata. Set include_content to true to also return the full HTML content.

tools.name: versions
tools.description: List version history of an artifact

findings.title: Lethal trifecta: untrusted input + private data + external sink
findings.detail: Server "mcp" exposes tools that together enable an exfiltration flow. Untrusted content: get · Private data: publish_site · Public sink: publish, publish_site.

evidence.snippet: Untrusted content: get · Private data: publish_site · Public sink: publish, publish_site

findings.remediation: Do not co-install these capabilities under one trust boundary; an injected comment/email can steer the model to exfiltrate via the sink tool.
